item.title: I’ve never heard this so eloquently explained
item.description: I intuitively knew this, but having it defined like this is blowing my mind. FP edit: I’m so glad others got as much from this post as I did! Can’t really take credit since all I did was see someone else’s post and think imgurians should see it. So…just be kind to yourself, I guess!
item.dc:creator: Chimaeraxxxxx

item.title: There is more to this than simple bullying.
item.dc:creator: Lanhdanan

item.title: 15 y.o. uses his toy drone to pinpoint an advancing Russian convoy… which was then wiped out by Kyiv's military
item.description:
A teenage civilian who used his drone to help Ukraine's armed forces locate and destroy a huge Russian convoy bearing down on Kyiv in the early days of the war has been hailed as a 'real hero of Ukraine'. Andrii Pokrasa, a 15-year-old boy who lives on the outskirts of the Ukrainian capital, was approached directly by the military to help them find the convoy, which was trundling along the E40 highway between Kyiv and Zhytomyr. Authorities approached Pokrasa as they knew he had purchased a consumer mini-drone last year and became a highly proficient pilot, Global News reported. Pokrasa duly took his drone into a nearby field under cover of darkness and successfully obtained photos and GPS co-ordinates of the oncoming convoy, which his father forwarded onto Ukrainian military via social media. Minutes later, Ukrainian artillery rained down on the highway near Berezivka and obliterated the convoy roughly 23 miles away from Kyiv city centre. The approach was so successful that the military handed the teenager the controls of a high-grade drone with a longer range and went on to destroy several Russian tank units and armoured vehicles as a result. '[Pokrasa] was the only one who was experienced with drones in that region,' Yurii Kasjanov, the commander of a territorial defence unit that liased with the teenage drone pilot, said. 'He's a real hero. A hero of Ukraine.'
Military-grade drones, such as the Turkish-made Bayraktar TB-2, have helped Ukraine's armed forces to inflict major losses on Russia's motorised units since the war began. But even simple consumer drones have proven invaluable in tracking Russian troop movements to inform military strategy calls and direct artillery fire. Commander Kasjanov said his territorial defence unit offered as much protection to Pokrasa and his family as they could while the teenager carried out his vital work. Pokrasa told Global News he was aware of the risks involved and was afraid, but said he knew 'I can't do it any other way'. The hotshot drone operator is one of many teenagers in Ukraine who are too young to join the military, but have contributed to the war effort by passing information to territorial defence units and working as lookouts. 'They feel themselves free people in a free land so that's why they want to be part of it,' Kasjanov said. Source: https://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-10893099/Teenager-hailed-hero-Ukraine-using-drone-pinpoint-Russian-convoy-destruction.html
Pokrasa described the experience as being 'very, very scary', but said he wanted to prevent the Russian forces from attacking his home town. '[The territorial defence force] provided us information on where approximately the Russian column could be. 'Our goal was to find the exact coordinates and provide the coordinates to the soldiers... I gave them the coordinates and photos, and after that they targeted the location,' the teenage pilot said. Ukraine's deployment of drones has been an integral part of their success in repelling Putin's forces from around its capital, and continues to be a highly-effective approach to fighting the invaders in the eastern Donbas region.
item.dc:creator: Igotfourbedrats

item.title: Nursing a Moose back to health
item.description:
Meet Moose. Last year he wandered up to our neighborhood covered in injuries, underweight, and worn out
We took him to the vet where we found out he had hookworms, fleas, ear mites, and heartworms. The poor guy was harboring entire ecosystems of parasites
The vet prescribed dewormer, flea meds, ear drops, Heartworm preventative, vaccines, and weeks of antibiotics to weaken the existing heartworms before he received the heartworm treatment shots.
Slowly his face healed and his fur grew back, the flea meds, ear drops, and dewormer did their jobs but he still had a tough road ahead with the heartworm treatment.
After he finished the antibiotics it was time for the heartworm shots. Three shots given on a set schedule with strict crate rest. No zoomies, no jumping, no excitement of any kind or a dead heartworm piece could cause a clot. The prospect of a potential “wormbolism” made the entire process pretty scary for me but he made it through the shots and the weeks of rest that followed.
It’s been almost a year since we started this process and he just went in for his 6 month follow up, the tests revealed he is now heartworm negative. TLDR: rescue dog Moose had injuries and all the parasites, now he’s healthy and likes to sleep on the couch with his feet in the air

item.title: Victim's Last Texts
item.description: VICTIM’S LAST TEXTS: The husband of Tulsa mass shooting victim Amanda Glenn has publicly released the last correspondence he had with his wife as a gunman opened fire in a medical clinic last week. Glenn, two doctors and a patient were killed before the suspect took his own life. Glenn’s husband says this is “why it’s so important to love one another and always say I love you anytime you’re leaving for any reason. She was our angel and now she has wings.”
item.dc:creator: mfrybeasley
